title: Kadane's algorithm: maximum subarray sum in O(n)
statement_informal: Kadane computes the maximum contiguous-subarray sum in one linear pass, although there are Theta(n^2) subarrays. Running variables cur (max prefix sum ending here, >=0) and best (max over all subarrays so far). Correctness: best l is an upper bound on every contiguous subarray's sum AND equals some subarray's sum — i.e. exactly the maximum.

cost_model.notes: One pass: cur (x::r) = max 0 (x + cur r), best (x::r) = max (cur (x::r)) (best r); O(1) work per element. Spec over Z: window i k l = firstn k (skipn i l); best is the max of Zsum over all windows.

references: J. Bentley, Programming Pearls — the maximum-subarray problem; Kadane's linear scan.
A linear-time dynamic program for a problem with quadratically many subarrays.
